description: CRUMB conformance fixture manifest. Implementations claiming v=1.1 conformance MUST pass every required fixture below. Tooling is expected to load this file, exercise each fixture, and compare against the declared expectation.

categories.valid: Inputs that MUST parse successfully and round-trip to the expected JSON.
categories.invalid: Inputs that MUST be rejected with a validation error matching the expected message.
categories.extensions: Inputs exercising namespaced extensions; parsers MUST accept them and ignore unknown headers/sections per SPEC §8.1.

fixtures.notes: Minimal task crumb with all three required sections.
Append-only log with [entries] in '- [ISO8601] text' form.
kind= header value is not in the allowed set.
Pack extension headers must be preserved verbatim and must not affect base parsing.
Bridge export extension; namespaced headers must round-trip without modification.

core.description: MUST pass all `valid` and `invalid` fixtures with required=true.

extensions.description: MUST pass all `core` fixtures plus every `extensions` fixture.

expected_format.valid: JSON file containing the canonical parsed shape: { headers: {...}, sections: { name: [lines] } }
expected_format.invalid: Plain text file containing the substring of the validation error message that the implementation MUST produce.
expected_format.extensions: Same shape as `valid`; namespaced headers appear under `headers` verbatim.

stability: This manifest follows the rules in SPEC.md §9 and docs/STABILITY.md. Fixture IDs are stable for the v=1.x line; new fixtures may be added in minor releases, none will be renamed or removed without a major bump.
